```{r}
        revised_base_description <- revised_base_label
      }
      
      # Update 'base_description' in the filtered dataset
      multis2 <- multis2 %>% mutate(base_description = revised_base_description)
      
      # Logical test: drop existing variable names from 'base_info'
      if (new_variable_name %in% names(base_info[[3]])) {
        
        keep_item_2 <- which(!(base_info[[2]] %>% names()) %in% new_variable_name)
        keep_item_3 <- which(!(base_info[[3]] %>% names()) %in% new_variable_name)
        keep_item_4 <- which(!(base_info[[4]] %>% names()) %in% new_variable_name)
        base_info[[2]] <- base_info[[2]][keep_item_2]
        base_info[[3]] <- base_info[[3]][keep_item_3]
        base_info[[4]] <- base_info[[4]][keep_item_4]

        # stop("Error: New multicode variable name already in data.")
      } 
      
      # Where base description provided, return a list containing:
      #  1. The updated 'multis2' dataframe
      #  2. The revised base label
      #  3. The new variable label
      return(list(multis2, revised_base_label, new_variable_label))
    
    } else {
      
      return(list(multis2)) # Otherwise, simply return data frame, in a list so map() below will work
      
    }
    
    }) # End of map() loop
  
  # Combine all first elements of 'result' (the dataframes) into one big dataframe
  tables <- bind_rows(map(result, ~(.x[[1]])))
  
  # Skip base description related steps where base_info NULL
  if(!is.null(base_info)){
    
    # Extract all base labels from the results, flatten into one vector, and remove duplicates
    new_base_descriptors <- map(result, ~(.x[[2]])) %>% unlist
    new_base_descriptors <- new_base_descriptors[!duplicated(new_base_descriptors)]
    
    # Extract all variable labels from the results, flatten, and remove duplicates
    new_variable_labels <- map(result, ~(.x[[3]])) %>% unlist
    new_variable_labels <- new_variable_labels[!duplicated(new_variable_labels)]
    
    # Make a temporary copy of 'base_info'
    example_of_base_info2_temp <- base_info
    
    # If those variable names already exist in base descriptions information, then drop them...
    base_info[[2]] <- base_info[[2]][which(!base_info[[2]] %in% new_base_descriptors)]
    base_info[[3]] <- base_info[[3]][which(!base_info[[3]] %in% new_variable_labels)]
    base_info[[4]] <- base_info[[4]][which(!base_info[[4]] %in% new_variable_labels)]

    # Append new base descriptors and labels to positions [[2]], [[3]], and [[4]] of that object
    example_of_base_info2_temp[[2]] <- c(base_info[[2]], new_base_descriptors)
    example_of_base_info2_temp[[3]] <- c(base_info[[3]], new_variable_labels)
    example_of_base_info2_temp[[4]] <- c(base_info[[4]], new_variable_labels)
    
    # Overwrite the original 'base_info' object in the global environment with the updated version
    assign(base_info_name_as_string, example_of_base_info2_temp, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    # base_info <<- example_of_base_info2_temp
  
  }
  
  # Create multicode object to export
  multis2 <- tables %>% 
    ungroup() %>% # Otherwise, cannot drop left_stem
    # Drop temporary variables
    select(-lev_num, -lev_relevant, -left_stem, -stem_count, -base_count, -left_stem2, -right_stem)
  
  # Bind single and multicode data together
  multicoded_and_single_code_data <- bind_rows(single_codes, multis2)
  
  return(multicoded_and_single_code_data)
  
  } else {return(data)}
  
}

write_function(convert_multicodes, folder = ddir)


```

<br>

#### Test multicode conversion {.tabset}

##### Test 1

```{r}

convert_multicodes(
  data = trial1, 
  base_info = example_of_base_information2, 
  keep = "Yes") %>% df_to_dense_flextable()

```

<br>

##### Test 2

```{r}
convert_multicodes(
  data = trial2, 
  base_info = example_of_base_information2, 
  keep) %>% 
  df_to_dense_flextable()

```

<br>

##### Test 3

```{r}
rm(base_info)
convert_multicodes(
  data = trial3 %>% mutate(base=NA,base_description=NA), 
  base_info = NULL,
  # base_info = example_of_base_information2, 
  keep) %>% 
  df_to_dense_flextable()

convert_multicodes(
  data = trial3, 
  base_info = example_of_base_information2,
  keep) %>% 
  df_to_dense_flextable()

```

<br><br><br>

## calc_stats final

The updated version of calc_stats below now incorporates some additional steps, over and above calc_stats further up in this script:

-   Multicode handling now dealt with within calc_stats itself.\
-   It also allows for different sets of predictors to be used with each outcome, if needed.\
-   I also now remembered to set `conf` to `NULL` by default.

```{r}
## For testing... 
# data <- df2
# outcomes <- "MuchPov"
# predictors <- "RSex"
# statistics <- "count"
# conf <- "se"
# base <- NULL
# 
# rm(data)
# rm(outcomes)
# rm(predictors)
# rm(statistics)
# rm(conf)
# rm(base)

```

<br>

### Handle nested predictors

```{r}

# Predictor variables which should change for each outcome 
# Here, suitable where two outcomes only
# Top level of predictor list needs to be same length as outcomes. 

nested_predictors <- 
list(
  list("MuchPov", "RSex", "RAgeCat"), 
  list(c("skipmeal", "RAgeCat"), "MuchPov2")
) 

predictors_one_level <- list(c("skipmeal", "RAgeCat"), "MuchPov2")
```

This function then returns list depth. If depth equals 1 then use same predictors for all outcomes. If depth equals 2 then try and use different sets of predictors for different outcomes.

```{r}

list_depth <- function(x) {
  if (!is.list(x)) {
    return(0)  # Not a list → depth 0
  } else if (length(x) == 0) {
    return(1)  # Empty list → depth 1
  } else {
    return(1 + max(sapply(x, list_depth)))
  }
}

write_function(list_depth, folder = ddir)

list_depth(nested_predictors)
list_depth(predictors_one_level)
```

This function is there to handle occasions where data has list depth of greater than 2, in these cases predictors are ignored (set to NULL). Also, if predictors are in a vector rather than a list they are put in a list (this is just a cleaning thing, it doesn't affect output).

```{r}
manage_predictors <- function(predictors, outcomes){
  
  if(is.null(predictors)){
    
    pred <- rep(list(predictors), length(outcomes))
    
    } else if (is.list(predictors)){
      
      ldepth <- list_depth(predictors)
      
      if(ldepth > 3){pred <- NULL} else {pred <- predictors}
      
    } else {
      
      pred <- list(predictors)
      
    }
  
  if(!is.null(predictors)){
  if(list_depth(pred) == 1){pred <- pred %>% list}
  
  pred_lenth <- length(pred)
  if(pred_lenth !=1  & pred_lenth != length(outcomes)){pred <- pred[1]}
  pred_lenth <- length(pred)
  if(pred_lenth == 1){pred <- rep(list(pred), length(outcomes)) %>% flatten}
  }
  
  return(pred)
  
}

write_function(manage_predictors, folder = ddir)

nested_predictors
manage_predictors(predictors = nested_predictors, outcomes = "RSex") 
manage_predictors(predictors = nested_predictors, outcomes = c("RSex", "libauth")) 
manage_predictors(predictors = nested_predictors, outcomes = c("RSex", "libauth", "welfare2"))

predictors_one_level
manage_predictors(predictors_one_level, outcomes = "RSex")
manage_predictors(predictors_one_level, outcomes = c("RSex", "libauth")) 

manage_predictors("RSex", outcomes = "RAgeCat")
manage_predictors("RSex", outcomes = c("RAgeCat", "libauth")) 

manage_predictors(NULL, outcomes = c("RAgeCat", "libauth")) 


```

<br>

### Revised function

```{r}

# outcomes <- c("RSex", "RAgeCat", "test")
# nested_predictors <- rev(nested_predictors)

calc_stats <- function(
    data,
    outcomes, # Character vector of outcome variables
    predictors = NULL, # a list, usually each element contains a character vector of predictor variables
    statistics = c("count","mean"),  # default, unweighted count or mean
    conf = NULL, # "se" or "ci"
    base = NULL, 
    pval = NULL # Whether to compute p-values (if not NULL)
    ){
  suppressMessages({suppressWarnings({
    
  # Where necessary, extract unweighted data from survey objects  
  if(any(str_detect(class(data), "survey"))){
    unweighted_data <- data[["variables"]]
    weighted_data <- data
  } else {
      unweighted_data <- data
      weighted_data <- NULL
  }
  
  # Organise predictors (see manage_predictors above)
  managed_preds <- manage_predictors(predictors = predictors, outcomes = outcomes) 
  
  # Compute statistics for each outcome/predictor combination 
  individual_tables <- pmap(list(outcomes, managed_preds), function(cur_outcome, predictor_set){
    
    # Extract current outcome variable and filter statistics based on variable type
    single_outcome <- unweighted_data %>% pull(cur_outcome) 
    if(is.factor(single_outcome)){statistic <- str_remove_all(statistics, "mean|median|sum")}
    if(is.numeric(single_outcome)){statistic <- str_remove_all(statistics, "perc|count")}
    
    results <- list() # Store stat results for this combo

    if("mean" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_mean(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictor_set, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("median" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_median(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictor_set, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("sum" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_sum(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictor_set, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("perc" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_perc(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictor_set, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("count" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(unweighted_count(unweighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictor_set, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    
    if(!is.null(weighted_data)){
    if("w_mean" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_mean(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictor_set, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("w_median" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_median(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictor_set, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("w_sum" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_sum(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictor_set, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("w_perc" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_perc(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictor_set, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    if("w_count" %in% statistic){results <- append(results, list(weighted_count(weighted_data, outcomes = cur_outcome, predictors = predictor_set, conf = conf, base = base)))}
    }
    
    # Combine results for this outcome/predictor
    if(length(results) > 0){results <- results %>% reduce(bind_rows)}

    return(results)
  
  })

  # Merge all results into one table 
  merged_tables <- individual_tables %>% bind_rows
  
  if(!is.null(pval)){
    
    # Extract predictors eligible for p-value testing (only single variables)
    pvalue_preds <- flatten(managed_preds) 
    pvalue_preds <- pvalue_preds[pvalue_preds %>% map_lgl(., ~length(.x) == 1)]
    pvalue_preds <- pvalue_preds %>% unlist %>% unique
    
  pvalues <- list()
  
  # Unweighted p-values
  if(any(statistics %in% c("mean","median","sum","perc","count"))){
    unw_pvalues <- map_return_p_values(data = unweighted_data, outcomes  = outcomes, predictors = pvalue_preds)
    pvalues <- append(pvalues, list(unw_pvalues))
  }

  # Weighted p-values
  if(!is.null(weighted_data)){
    if(any(statistics %in% c("w_mean", "w_median", "w_sum", "w_perc", "w_count"))){
      wgt_pvalues <- map_return_p_values(data = data, outcomes  = outcomes, predictors = pvalue_preds)
      pvalues <- append(pvalues, list(wgt_pvalues))
      }
  }
  
  # Merge p-values into main results
  pvalues <- pvalues %>% bind_rows()
  merged_tables <- merged_tables %>% left_join(pvalues)
  
  } else { 
    # If no p-values requested, still add empty columns
    merged_tables <- merged_tables %>% mutate(p_method = NA, p_value = NA)
  }
  
  # Handle the case of no predictors (total summary) 
  if(is.null(predictors)){merged_tables <- merged_tables %>% mutate(cross_break = "Total")}
  
  # Convert multi-coded values using base info
  merged_tables <- convert_multicodes(
    data = merged_tables,  
    base_info = base, 
    keep = "Yes"
    ) 

  return(merged_tables)
  
})})
}

write_function(calc_stats, folder = ddir)

```

<br>

#### Tests {.tabset}

##### Test 1

```{r}

test1 <- calc_stats(data = df_svy, 
           outcomes = c("RAgeCat","libauth", "welfare3"), 
           predictors = list(c("RSex","skipmeal2","MuchPov2"), "RSex"),
           statistics = c("w_perc","mean"), 
           base = example_of_base_information,
           conf = "ci", pval = T) 


test1 %>% print(n=1000)

test1 %>% pull(p_value)

```

<br>

##### Test 2

```{r}
calc_stats(data = df2, 
           outcomes = list("libauth","welfare2", "leftrigh"), 
           predictors = list("RSex", "RAgeCat", "MuchPov2"), 
           pval = T) %>% df_to_dense_flextable()


```

<br>

##### Test 3

```{r}
calc_stats(data = df2, 
           outcomes = c("libauth","welfare2", "leftrigh"), 
           predictors = list("RSex", "RAgeCat", "MuchPov2"), 
           pval = T) %>% 
  print(n=1000)

```

<br>

##### Test 4.

```{r}

calc_stats(data = df2, 
           outcomes = c("libauth","welfare2", "leftrigh"), 
           predictors = list("RSex", "RAgeCat", "MuchPov2", c("RSex", "MuchPov2")),
           pval = T) %>%
  print(n=1000)


```

<br>

##### Test 5

```{r}

calc_stats(data = df2, 
           outcomes = c("libauth","welfare2"), 
           predictors = nested_predictors,
           pval = T) %>% df_to_dense_flextable()

```

<br>

##### Test 6

```{r}

trial2 <- calc_stats(
    survey_data, 
    base = example_of_base_information2, 
    outcomes = c("Q1_OptionA", "Q1_OptionB", "Q1_OptionC", "Q1_OptionD", "Q1_OptionE", 
                 "Q2_ChoiceA", "Q2_ChoiceB", "Q2_ChoiceC", "Q2_ChoiceD", "Q2_ChoiceE", 
                 "Satisfaction"),
    predictors = "AgeGroup",
    conf = "se") 

trial2

```

##### Test 7

```{r}

test7 <- calc_stats(data = df_svy, 
           outcomes = c("RAgeCat","libauth", "welfare3"), 
           ## predictors = list(c("RSex","skipmeal2","MuchPov2")),
           statistics = c("w_perc","mean"), 
           base = example_of_base_information,
           conf = "ci") %>% print(n=1000)

```
